subject: Project Management
title: The Fat-Tail Distribution
description: A fast breakdown of The Fat-Tail Distribution for builders.

hook.text: Project overruns don't follow a bell curve. They follow a fat-tailed distribution — extreme outcomes happen more often.

buildup.text: A 'normal' delay might be 20%. But fat tails mean 200% or 500% overruns happen far more than expected.

discovery.text: IT projects have the fattest tails: 1 in 6 has a cost overrun of over 200%.

twist.text: Standard risk analysis assumes normal distributions. In projects, the extremes are where the real danger lives.

climax.text: Fat tails mean project managers should fear the unlikely scenario more than they typically do.

punchline.text: In projects, the 'unlikely' disaster happens all the time.

quiz.question: What does a 'fat-tailed distribution' mean for projects?
options: Extreme overruns happen far more often than a normal distribution predicts
Costs always stay within 20% of budget
